text: This weekend's tornado left downed trees, broken limbs, and debris across Fort Worth properties. If your yard took damage, do not wait to address it. Hanging branches and partially uprooted trees are a safety hazard, especially with more storms in the forecast. Our emergency tree care crew is actively working storm cleanup across Fort Worth and the surrounding areas this week. We handle full tree removal, limb clearing, stump grinding, and debris hauling. If you have storm damage on your property, call (817) 995-0324. We are prioritizing emergency calls and scheduling assessments as fast as we can get to them.

Your irrigation system sat dormant all winter. Before you turn it on for the season, it needs a full inspection. Cracked heads, broken valves, and line leaks from freeze cycles are common across Fort Worth properties this time of year. Our crews run a zone-by-zone diagnostic, checking coverage, pressure, and controller programming. One missed leak can waste thousands of gallons and drown a section of turf before you notice. Schedule a sprinkler inspection now, before the summer watering schedule starts.

Drainage problems don't wait for a convenient time. Standing water after a spring storm, erosion along a foundation, or soggy turf that won't dry out all point to the same thing: water with nowhere to go. Our drainage team installs French drains, catch basins, and regrading solutions across Fort Worth and the surrounding cities. Every project starts with a site evaluation to trace where water collects and map the path it needs to follow. If your yard holds water after rain, that is not normal. Call (817) 995-0324 for a drainage assessment.

A patio, walkway, or retaining wall changes how you use your outdoor space. Our hardscaping crews work with flagstone, pavers, natural stone, and concrete to build structures that hold up to the Fort Worth heat and clay soil movement. Every hardscape project starts with a grade and drainage check, because a patio that pools water is worse than no patio at all. We handle the full scope: excavation, base prep, material selection, and installation. Currently scheduling hardscape projects in Fort Worth, Aledo, and Southlake for May and June.

May is when your lawn either gets set up for summer or falls behind. Bermuda and zoysia are coming out of dormancy, and the next 4 weeks determine how they perform through July and August. Our crews are running spring aeration, applying slow-release fertilizer, and adjusting mowing heights across Fort Worth, Keller, and Colleyville properties. If your turf still looks thin or patchy from winter, now is the time to address it. Once temperatures hit 100, your options shrink. Schedule a lawn assessment while there is still time to correct course.

New sod transforms a yard in a single day, but the prep work underneath is what makes it last. Our crews start every sod installation with soil testing, grade correction, and irrigation verification. We install Bermuda, St. Augustine, and Zoysia varieties matched to your property's sun exposure and foot traffic. Fort Worth's Blackland Prairie clay soil requires specific prep to avoid drainage issues under new turf. If you are dealing with bare spots, dead grass from winter, or a yard that needs a full reset, call (817) 995-0324 for a property assessment.

Landscape lighting does two things: it extends the usable hours of your outdoor space and it adds a layer of security to your property. Our lighting installations cover pathways, bed accents, uplighting on trees and architectural features, and perimeter security lighting. Every fixture is low-voltage LED, wired to a programmable transformer with dusk-to-dawn automation. We run all wiring underground and through conduit where it crosses hardscape, so nothing is exposed to mowers or foot traffic. Placement matters more than quantity. A few well-positioned fixtures create depth and visibility without washing out the yard. We map light zones during a site visit, accounting for existing trees, structures, and how the property is used at night. If your front entry is dark, your walkways disappear after sunset, or you want to highlight landscaping you already invested in, call (817) 995-0324 for a lighting assessment.

A sprinkler system that ran fine last year can have three or four issues hiding by spring. Cracked PVC from freeze cycles, heads knocked out of alignment by foot traffic, and valve diaphragms that stick open or closed are the most common problems we find across Fort Worth properties. Our irrigation technician runs a full zone-by-zone diagnostic before recommending any repairs. That means pressurizing each zone, checking head spray patterns, measuring flow rates, and inspecting the controller programming. We replace heads, rebuild valves, repair main and lateral line breaks, and reprogram controllers to match current city watering schedules. Fort Worth's clay soil shifts constantly, which puts stress on buried PVC lines and fittings that rigid systems were not designed to handle. If you are seeing dry spots, soggy areas, or a water bill that jumped without explanation, those are signs of a system that needs attention. Call (817) 995-0324 to schedule an irrigation diagnostic.

Commercial property maintenance is about consistency. Tenants, visitors, and property managers notice when a landscape crew misses details or skips a week. Our commercial teams run defined checklists on every visit: mow, edge, blow, spot-treat weeds, and document with photos. We service retail centers, apartment communities, office parks, and municipal properties across Fort Worth. Every property gets a dedicated crew on a fixed schedule so the same people are on your site every time. That means they know the property, they know the problem areas, and they catch issues before they become complaints. We are currently servicing over 500 properties with a 98% client retention rate. If your current provider is inconsistent or you are managing multiple vendor relationships, call (817) 995-0324 to discuss a single-source maintenance contract.

DFW temps are climbing into the 90s, and most sprinkler systems are about to get pushed hard for the first time this season. Before the heat sets in, a few checks make a real difference. Walk every zone with the system running and confirm each head pops up fully and sprays where it should, not onto the driveway or the fence. Look for soft spots in the lawn that signal a buried line leak. Bump your controller from spring settings to summer with longer cycles, fewer days, and run times before sunrise to cut evaporation loss. Heads typically need replacement every 5 to 7 years, and Fort Worth's clay soil shifts crack PVC faster than people expect. If you are not sure where your system stands heading into June, a zone-by-zone diagnostic now costs less than a heat-stressed lawn in July. Call (817) 995-0324 to schedule a sprinkler inspection.

Memorial Day weekend is one week away, and Fort Worth yards are about to be the backdrop for cookouts, family gatherings, and the first real weekend of summer. We want to take a moment to recognize the veterans and service members across our community who carry the weight that the rest of us get to enjoy this weekend. If you are getting your property ready for guests, a clean cut, fresh edging, and blown-out beds make a bigger difference than people realize. Our crews are running full schedules to make sure every property is presentation-ready before the weekend. We are proud to serve Fort Worth, Aledo, Keller, Colleyville, and the surrounding communities. From all of us at Black Hill Landscaping, have a safe and meaningful Memorial Day weekend.
